page: Microsoft Azure EC8ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECADSv5 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server includes 307 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for integration with high-performance external storage volumes. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware, focusing its resource profile entirely on CPU and memory density.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC8ads_v5 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute allocation, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial RAM without the overhead of a higher vCPU count. Appropriate workloads for this configuration include memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that fit within its 64.0 GB memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the ECADSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. For storage, the instance includes 307 GB of local disk space and supports Premium Storage. It does not feature integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as relational databases, caching systems, and data processing applications, where memory capacity is prioritized over high compute core counts. The combination of dedicated AMD CPU resources and local storage capacity provides a stable environment for transactional databases and memory-bound enterprise applications.
